summary: A tiny Mediterranean country in total economic and political collapse, dominated by Hezbollah (an Iranian-backed militia with more weapons than the Lebanese army), devastated by the 2020 Beirut explosion and years of sectarian paralysis.

history.detail: France draws borders including Christian, Sunni, Shia, Druze communities.
Power-sharing agreement along sectarian lines; President (Christian), PM (Sunni), Speaker (Shia).
15-year war involving Palestinian factions, Syria, Israel, militias; ~150,000 dead.
Israel invades to expel PLO; Iran helps found Hezbollah in response.
34-day war between Israel and Hezbollah; 1,200 Lebanese civilians killed.
Currency loses 95% of value; banks freeze accounts.
2,750 tonnes of ammonium nitrate explodes; 218 dead, 300,000 homeless. Negligence confirmed.
Nasrallah and much of Hezbollah leadership killed; massive strikes on Beirut.

finance.currency_role: Lebanese Pound (LBP) — collapsed; multiple exchange rates
finance.central_bank: Banque du Liban (1964) — accused of Ponzi scheme

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Effectively destroyed by economic crisis and Beirut explosion

resources.oil_gas: Offshore gas blocks (Karish disputed with Israel); Block 9 with France/Italy/Russia consortium

follow_the_money.detail: Lebanese diaspora (~16M vs 5M at home) sends $6-7B/yr — lifeline during collapse
Iranian funding + drug trafficking + business empire; US-designated terror financing

theories_disputed.detail: Official: criminal negligence storing ammonium nitrate. Some allege deliberate sabotage; investigation blocked by political interference.
UN Special Tribunal convicted Hezbollah member in absentia; Hezbollah denies; Syria also implicated.

connections.detail: Hezbollah funded and armed by Iran's IRGC
Multiple wars; ongoing border skirmishes; 2024 major strikes
French cultural influence; France leads international mediation
